name: Morphosis Capital Coöperatief U.A
financingStages: Growth
industrySector: Business Products and Services; Consumer Goods and Services; ; Biotech and Healthcare Niche Manufacturing
profile:
Morphosis Capital (MC), established by four Romanian entrepreneurs, is a private equity fund manager focused on transforming local SMEs into national and regional champions. The firm operates through two funds: MC Fund I (~€50M fully invested capital) and MC Fund II (+€130M committed capital).

With the support of EIF as anchor investor, MC Fund I has deployed tickets between €5-10M across 6 platform investments in sectors including healthcare, technology, and B2B services. The portfolio comprises DocProcess (business process automation), Medima Health (imaging clinics), Dr. Leahu Dental Clinics (dental market leader), EMI (engineering services), Cronos Med (aesthetic medicine), and Stay Fit Gym (fitness operator). 

MC Fund II, launched in challenging market conditions, already secured a total capital commitment of +€130M, with support from EIF, EBRD, IFC and private investors. The fund plans 9-10 investments (consisting of €10-15M ticket size) in industries such as healthcare, consumer services, B2B services, and niche manufacturing. While maintaining a 70% focus on Romania, Fund II will expand its geographical scope into other CEE markets including Bulgaria, Slovakia, Croatia, Poland and Czech Republic. MC Fund II has already completed 3 investments: Romania Education Alliance (private education institution with dual curriculum), Supermarket La Cocos (the largest independent hard discount retailer in Romania) and EnduroSat (provider of satellites and space services).

As a publicly listed European investment company, Gimv leverages a strong balance sheet, patient capital, deep local roots, and a global outlook to help build leading companies that address today’s economic and social challenges. 
We work hand-in-hand with our portfolio companies to foster sustainable growth and create long-term value for future generations. As a strategic partner, we support entrepreneurship, drive expansion, and reinforce long-term strategies, aiming to help companies become sector leaders.
Gimv operates across five forward-looking investment domains: Consumer, Healthcare, Life Sciences, Smart Industries, and Sustainable Cities. Through these platforms, we back companies at various growth stages and across diverse sectors. Currently, our portfolio includes around 60 companies, collectively generating over €4.5 billion in revenue and employing more than 20,000 professionals.
Additionally, in partnership with WorxInvest, Gimv invests via Gimv Anchor Investment, focusing on high-quality, often established businesses with strong market positions and stable cash flows. Acting as a sustainable shareholder, we support these companies’ next strategic steps while respecting their unique identity and entrepreneurial spirit.

name: Extantia Capital Management GmbH
financingStages: Growth; Seed; Start-up
industrySector: Climate Tech
profile:
Extantia is a leading venture capital fund that invests in breakthrough climate technologies. Our goal is to generate a financial return on our investments, while each of them should have the potential to significantly reduce carbon emissions.We focus on those sectors that produce the most GHG emissions, which currently are energy generation, industry, buildings, transportation, agriculture, and waste.
With our rigorous carbon math process, we assess the potential GHG reductions a technology can provide. We invest only if the technology has a direct impact of reducing at least 100 megatons (100 million tons) of CO2e/year or an indirect impact as a significant enabling force that allows other sectors to decarbonize.

profile:
ECBF is the first venture fund exclusively dedicated to the (circular) bioeconomy. We invest in visionary European entrepreneurs who are driving the shift from a fossil-based to a bio-based economy.

ECBF aims to catalyze the transition towards a sustainable future by investing in biobased growth-stage companies with high potential for innovation, favorable returns, and sustainable impact. As a growth-stage venture capital fund, ECBF syndicates with private and public investors to bring circular technologies and bio-products to market, offering flexible financing tools from equity to mezzanine.

With a total of EUR 300M under management, ECBF is focused on deploying capital on attractive and impactful companies based in Europe and active in AgTech, FoodTech, Industrial Biotech and Biobased Materials (Packaging and Construction BioMaterials). Simultaneously, ECBF shares its industry expertise to raise awareness among private and public investors to increase their financial participation in bioeconomy companies.

CapMan is headquartered in Helsinki and has offices in Stockholm, Copenhagen, Oslo, London and Luxembourg. We are a public company listed on Nasdaq Helsinki since 2001 and a signatory of the UN Principles for Responsible Investment (PRI) since 2012.

profile:
Capital D is a mid-market private equity firm focused on backing disruptive, profitable and fast-growing businesses in Europe. The firm brings a buyout mindset to growth investing, partnering with founder-led companies at or near profitability. 

Capital D typically invests €20-60 million, taking control or co-control positions with limited use of leverage. The firm targets concentrated portfolios of 6 to 8 investments per fund, with a 3 to 5 year holding period, supporting companies through hands-on value creation. Dedicated Operating Partners work alongside management teams to accelerate growth, strengthen leadership, scale go-to-market strategies, enhance technology and data capabilities, and position businesses for strategic exits.

Capital D has built a strong track record of identifying winners early, delivering attractive returns, and embedding ESG best practices within its portfolio companies.

name: Ambienta SGR S.p.A.
financingStages: Mid market buyout (€15m-€150m equity)
industrySector: Industry agnostic, exclusive focus on environmental sustainability
profile:
Ambienta is a European environmental sustainability investor across private equity, public markets and private credit. Operating out of Milan, London, Paris and Munich, Ambienta manages over €4.0bn in assets with a focus on investing in private and public companies driven by environmental megatrends and whose products or services improve Resource Efficiency or Pollution Control. In private equity, Ambienta has completed 80 Investments to date. In public equity markets, Ambienta has pioneered one of the world's largest absolute return funds entirely focused on environmental sustainability and manages a full suite of sustainable products ranging from low-risk multi-asset funds to equity long-only. Ambienta has also recently established a private credit strategy with the same environmental sustainability focus as the other asset classes. 
An industry pioneer, Ambienta was one of the first UN PRI signatories in 2012 and attained B-Corp status in 2019. In 2020, Ambienta became IIGCC member and in 2023, as one of very few asset managers, continued being a positive role model for the industry by committing to the Science-Based Targets initiative (SBTi).

Meanings Capital Partners is a leading unlisted French investment platform specialising in the Lower Mid Cap segment in Private Equity, Infrastructure, and Real Estate.

Meanings assists management teams and, together with them, transforms and finances the growth of French businesses to empower them to become leaders on their markets. Meanings also transforms property assets and improves their use in line with the highest environmental standards.  

The platform is comprised of over 45 people organised around four divisions:

● Infrastructure, for companies with revenue of up to €150 million, 
● Private Equity Small Cap, for companies with revenue of up to €50 million,

profile:
Lakestar is one of the leading pan-European venture capital firms. Lakestar’s mission is to find, fund and grow disruptive businesses that are enabled by technology and founded by exceptional entrepreneurs in Europe and beyond. Founded by Klaus Hommels, the team’s early investments include Skype, Spotify, Facebook and Airbnb. Since raising its first fund in 2012, Lakestar manages an aggregated volume of over €2bn across four early stage funds and two growth funds.

The team actively advises and supports portfolio companies in marketing, recruitment, technology, product development and regulatory insight, accompanying founders from seed to early stage, growth stage or exit. Lakestar currently has the privilege of holding investments in Neko Health, Revolut, GetYourGuide, sennder, Eigen Technologies, Public.com, Builder.Ai, Yapily, Terra Quantum, Isar Aerospace, accuRx, Rhino and Auterion to name a few. A long-standing champion for Europe’s digital sovereignty, Lakestar has a presence in London, Berlin and Zurich with a team of 35 talented individuals from 18 countries.

Specialized provider of private equity investments. Founded in 1997, Schroder Adveq is a leading asset manager investing in private equity globally and since 2017, a wholly owned subsidiary of Schroders plc. Offering specialised investment solutions which enable our clients to access select private market segments through primary, secondary and direct/co-investments.
Schroder Adveq’s client base comprises institutional investors such as pension funds and insurance companies, located in Europe, North America, and the Asia-Pacific region. Many of them are repeat, long-term clients with whom the firm has developed a role as a trusted partner.
Schroder Adveq has offices in Zurich, Frankfurt, London, Jersey, New York, Beijing, and Hong Kong.
